github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGenerat.text:
Generate the commit message strictly following the Conventional Commits specification (https://www.conventionalcommits.org). Required structure:

  <type>(<scope>): <short description in imperative, ≤72 chars>
  <blank line>
  <optional body: what changed and why, not how>
  <blank line>
  <optional footer: issue references, breaking changes>

Allowed types:
  feat     → new feature
  fix      → bug fix
  style    → visual changes / CSS / SCSS without logic
  refactor → restructuring without behavior change
  content  → content updates / text / images
  chore    → maintenance tasks, dependencies, config
  docs     → documentation
  perf     → performance improvement

Scope: affected component in lowercase, e.g.:
  layout, tiles, nav, scss, js, pages, aves, wildlife, banner, config

Writing rules:
  - Description in English, imperative mood ('add', 'fix', 'move')
  - No period at the end of the first line
  - If there is a BREAKING CHANGE, indicate it in the footer: 'BREAKING CHANGE: <description>'
  - If it resolves an issue: 'Closes #<number>'
  - The body must answer 'why?' not 'how?'
  - Be concise but complete; avoid generic messages like 'fix bugs' or 'update files'

Examples of good short descriptions:
  style(scss): increase drop cap size to 5em on desktop
  feat(aves): add redirect pages for 6 species with /ave/ permalink
  refactor(nav): centralize navigation order in _config.yml
  content(wildlife): replace fauna list with links to SEO BirdLife pages
